name: Dornaque to Peña del Hierro Viewpoint Trail
name_original: Mirador Peña El Hierro – running circular por los Pinares de Rodeno
description: This easy 2.9-mile (4.6 km) jogging route through Pinares de Rodeno features pine forests, red sandstone, and the Peña El Hierro Viewpoint.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Bezas offer diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Albarracín, characterized by extensive pine forests and striking red sandstone formations. The region provides a scenic backdrop for outdoor activities, with well-maintained trails winding through natural attractions. Joggers can explore ancient pine forests and intriguing rock structures, encountering varying terrain from accessible paths to more challenging routes. Best jogging routes around Bezas The most popular jogging route is Dornaque to Peña del Hierro Viewpoint Trail, a 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes about 33 minutes to complete. This easy route leads through ancient pine forests to a panoramic viewpoint. Another top favourite among local runners is Jabalon and Ermita de San Cristobal - running from Jabaloyas, a moderate 7.1 miles (11.4 km) path. This route offers a challenging run with significant elevation gain towards Jabalón Peak. Local runners also love the Lázaro Shelter loop from El Navazo, a 5.5 miles (8.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 6 minutes. Jogging around Bezas is defined by extensive pine forests, distinctive red sandstone formations, and elevated viewpoints. The network of running routes offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more difficult trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 70 runners have used komoot to explore Bezas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Bezas?
A: There are over 10 dedicated jogging routes around Bezas listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for runners of all levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Bezas?
A: Jogging routes in Bezas primarily feature extensive pine forests and striking red sandstone formations. You'll encounter varied terrain, from well-maintained paths suitable for easy runs to more challenging trails with significant elevation gain, especially around areas like Jabalón Peak.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Bezas offers easy running options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983280110/dornaque-to-pena-del-hierro-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dornaque to Peña del Hierro Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.9-mile (4.6 km) route that leads through ancient pine forests to a panoramic viewpoint, perfect for a relaxed jog.

Q: Can I find longer distance running trails in Bezas?
A: Absolutely. For those looking for a longer run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24542005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navazo picnic area loop from El Navazo&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging 8.7-mile (14 km) route with notable elevation gain, providing a good workout amidst scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes in the Bezas area?
A: Yes, many routes in Bezas are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31447673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lázaro Shelter loop from El Navazo&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 5.5-mile (8.9 km) circular trail that takes you through varied terrain.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints I can reach while running in Bezas?
A: The region is known for its stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983280110/dornaque-to-pena-del-hierro-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dornaque to Peña del Hierro Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads directly to the Peña del Hierro Viewpoint, offering panoramic views. Additionally, the Jabalón Peak, accessible via routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983268958/jabalon-and-ermita-de-san-cristobal-running-from-jabaloyas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jabalon and Ermita de San Cristobal - running from Jabaloyas&lt;/a&gt;, provides magnificent views from its 1692-meter summit.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the running routes in Bezas?
A: While jogging, you can encounter several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/756481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas&lt;/a&gt;, the largest lagoon in Sierra de Albarracín, is a refreshing spot within the Pinares de Rodeno. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arquillo de San Blas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, a pleasant area for recreation.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the jogging trails?
A: Yes, the area offers cultural diversions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3930079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prado del Navazo Bulls Rock Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site featuring Levantine Rock Art, is a significant historical landmark. The Hermitage of San Cristóbal, located near Jabalón Peak, also adds a cultural touch to the landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Bezas?
A: Bezas offers enjoyable running conditions throughout much of the year. The extensive pine forests provide shade in summer, while the changing water levels of Laguna de Bezas create dynamic natural spectacles in other seasons. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Experienced runners will find several challenging options. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24542575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abrigo de Lázaro loop from Gea de Albarracín&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.8-mile (14.2 km) trail, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983268958/jabalon-and-ermita-de-san-cristobal-running-from-jabaloyas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jabalon and Ermita de San Cristobal - running from Jabaloyas&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.1-mile (11.4 km) path, feature significant elevation gains and varied terrain to test your endurance.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Bezas?
A: Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, particularly the ancient pine forests and striking red sandstone formations, which provide a scenic backdrop. The variety of trails catering to different fitness levels and the well-maintained paths are also frequently highlighted.
tips: This panoramic point will surprise you with the environment and the landscape that surrounds it. Located very close to the town of Bezas, you can get here by several paths walking through the ancient pine forests and once here enjoy the curious rock formations of this wonderful place.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Mirador Peña El Hierro jogging route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is rated as easy for jogging. It spans approximately 4.6 kilometers with about 100 meters of accumulated elevation gain, making it suitable for individuals of most fitness levels, including beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a mixed terrain. You'll primarily encounter natural paths (about 2.19 km) and unpaved sections (around 274 m). There's also a small portion of asphalt (130 m). While easy, some parts can be quite stony, so appropriate footwear is recommended to protect your feet and ankles.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the Mirador Peña El Hierro route?
faqs.answer: You'll jog through beautiful pine forests and encounter distinctive red sandstone formations. The area is rich in archaeological and geological interest, with large sandstone blocks that house ancient rock paintings and unique geological features like Liesegang rings. Don't miss the Peña El Hierro Viewpoint for scenic vistas.

faqs.question: Is there parking available near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, parking is generally available near the trailhead for routes within the Pinares de Rodeno area. Specific parking details might vary, but it's usually accessible by car to reach the starting point of this circular jog.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Pinares de Rodeno is a natural area, specific regulations for dogs on this particular trail can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Pinares de Rodeno area?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Paisaje Protegido de los Pinares de Rodeno (Protected Landscape of Pinares de Rodeno). Generally, there are no entrance fees or permits required for recreational activities like jogging on established trails within protected landscapes in Spain, but it's always good practice to respect the natural environment and follow any posted regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The Pinares de Rodeno area offers pleasant conditions for jogging during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, especially in sunny sections, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable. Winter jogging is possible, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially damp conditions.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular jogging route intersects with sections of other official trails. You'll find parts of the MontañasVacías (12% of the route), TE-67 (12%), and Sendero Accesible Dornaque - Fuente Buena (2%) overlapping with this path.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for a jog on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the mixed and sometimes stony terrain, we recommend sturdy trail running shoes. Dress in layers suitable for the weather, and bring water, especially on warmer days. A small backpack for essentials like snacks, a phone, and a basic first-aid kit is also a good idea.

faqs.question: Is this the same 'Mirador de la Peña' trail located on El Hierro island?
faqs.answer: No, this is a common point of confusion. This route, "Mirador Peña El Hierro - running circular por los Pinares de Rodeno," is located in the Pinares de Rodeno area, likely near Albarracín, Spain. It is distinct from the more challenging "Mirador de la Peña" hike found on the island of El Hierro, which is a different location with a much longer and more strenuous trail.
